title: Undercut and traffic-pressure features from same-lap opponents
summary: F1 strategy sources repeatedly emphasize that pit timing depends on undercut range, clean air, and traffic windows, not just tire age.
rationale: F1 strategy sources repeatedly emphasize that pit timing depends on undercut range, clean air, and traffic windows, not just tire age. Even without full gap telemetry, same-race-lap relative context can proxy whether a driver is vulnerable to an undercut or can rejoin into traffic.
implementation_hint: Within each Race-Year-LapNumber, create leakage-safe cross-sectional features from current-lap observations only: percentile/rank of LapTime_Delta, TyreLife, and degradation; local neighborhood medians by adjacent Position bands; leader-to-midfield pace separation; indicators for being slower than nearby position rivals on older tires; and interaction terms with Position and Compound. Avoid any future-lap aggregates.
expected_effect: Should help separate true pit-window pressure from generic late-stint wear by capturing strategic context against nearby rivals.
risk: Because direct time gaps are unavailable, proxies may be weak or track-specific. Cross-sectional aggregates must be computed per current lap only.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
